wound_trigger: CMMC Level 2 mandatory for all DoD primes by Nov 10, 2026
mechanism_description: Per-controller drift detection → on-device inference → cross-OEM fleet learning → defense-grade AI substrate that compounds with every shipped unit.
advantages: CMMC Level 2 compliance — mandatory for DoD primes by Nov 10, 2026. Most competitors are 12–24 months behind.
35-year installed base of defense-qualified controllers — unique training corpus no upstart can clone.
Family-owned governance — decision horizon measured in decades, not quarters. Capital patience is your moat.

engines.name: Control Modules
engines.flywheel_loop: CL-4002 drift → on-device inference → cross-OEM fleet learning → attached AI revenue

engines.name: Displays
engines.flywheel_loop: NPU-powered CL-715/716 → operator-state monitoring → contextual alerts → OEM support calls drop

engines.name: Keypads
engines.flywheel_loop: AI-assisted Keypad Builder → adaptive firmware → OEM design time drops 80% → workflow data compounds

threats.name: Parker Hannifin
threats.key_fact: Public AI strategy plus capital to acquire a tier-2 competitor

threats.name: Bosch Rexroth + Trackunit

threats.name: Grayhill / APEM

Engineering leadership treats AI as a threat instead of a force multiplier — and won't move.

wound_trigger: Replicator 2 program contracts close Q3 2027; non-Lattice-compatible bidders lose pre-qualification
capability_gap: Each platform sells, but the substrate underneath them isn't packaged as an enterprise sale to allies.
mechanism_description: Strip Lattice down to an ally-grade sovereign substrate — Australia, UK, Japan run their own instance on their own metal, Anduril licenses the runtime instead of selling hardware seat-by-seat.
advantages: You are the only US autonomy vendor whose stack already runs in 4+ allied nations — competitors quote 18-24 month delivery.
Lattice OS is the only AI-native C2 stack with production deployment counts higher than DARPA pilot scale.
Founder-led capital base (Founders Fund + sovereign LPs) gives you decision tempo Lockheed cannot match.

engines.name: Lattice Sovereign
engines.flywheel_loop: Each ally deployment → mission telemetry → federated training → Lattice model improves for every other ally

engines.name: Autonomous Manufacturing
engines.flywheel_loop: Arsenal-1 → tooling IP → ally co-production licensing → multi-country supply resilience

industry: Payment infrastructure and embedded financial services
wound_trigger: OpenAI, Anthropic, and 3 hyperscalers will pick a default rails partner by EOY 2026
capability_gap: You sit under every AI startup's revenue, but you're not the financial substrate for AI usage itself.
mechanism_description: Stripe becomes the metering, settlement, and audit layer for token-based AI billing across every model provider and every enterprise buyer — a Visa for inference.
advantages: You already process billions in AI startup revenue — the seller side is solved.
Stripe Treasury + Issuing means you can settle and re-meter inside the same primitive.
Privately held, founder-led, no quarterly earnings call to satisfy — long-horizon plays available.

engines.name: Inference Metering
engines.flywheel_loop: Every token billed → usage corpus → metering becomes pricing infra → model providers default to Stripe

engines.name: AI Treasury
engines.flywheel_loop: AI startups → automated FBO accounts → predictable cashflow → embedded credit lines compound

engines.name: Audit Substrate
engines.flywheel_loop: Enterprise AI buyers → SOX-compliant token receipts → audit becomes Stripe-native → moat hardens

threats.name: Adyen
threats.key_fact: Already moving on enterprise AI metering; deeper non-US bank relationships

threats.name: AWS Marketplace + Bedrock billing
threats.key_fact: Native settlement inside hyperscaler buying motion; Stripe locked out by design

threats.name: OpenAI ChatGPT Enterprise direct-billing
threats.key_fact: Vertical integration risk — model providers cut out the rails entirely

disqualifiers: You believe payments commoditization protects you. It doesn't in this segment.
You're not willing to build settlement primitives outside USD.
Product leadership treats AI billing as a vertical instead of a platform.

industry: Enterprise data labeling and weak supervision for AI
wound_trigger: Scale AI's Meta acquisition closes Q4 2026, eliminating you from any Meta-aligned enterprise account
capability_gap: You have the weak supervision IP, but the enterprise procurement story is still 'data labeling' — not 'AI substrate'.
mechanism_description: Convert Snorkel from a labeling tool into a compiler that takes domain rules → produces fine-tuned weights → ships into a customer's private model registry; sold as substrate not service.
advantages: Founder team's Stanford research (Ratner, Ré) is the canonical citation in 80% of weak supervision papers — durable academic moat.
You are the only commercial vendor with production deployments at JPMorgan, Pixar, Wayfair, and BNY Mellon simultaneously.
Series D capital base lets you pivot from labeling SaaS to AI compiler without dilution.

engines.name: Domain-Rule Compiler
engines.flywheel_loop: Enterprise rule corpus → adapter weights → customer fine-tunes → returns telemetry → compiler improves

engines.name: Vertical Foundations
engines.flywheel_loop: Per-industry pre-trained adapters → market-of-one substrate → adapter store flywheel

engines.name: Governance Substrate

wound_trigger: ChatGPT shopping, Perplexity buy-buttons, and Amazon Rufus reroute >20% of merchant traffic by H2 2026
capability_gap: Your AI features are merchant-side; you haven't packaged the buyer-side AI rails yet.
mechanism_description: Every AI shopping agent (ChatGPT, Perplexity, Rufus) routes through a Shopify Buyer Adapter — Shopify becomes the AI-shopping rails, merchants get traffic protection, agents get clean SKU data.
advantages: You already host 10% of all global ecommerce — agent platforms need your SKU graph more than you need them.
Shop App is the only commerce-native buyer app with 100M+ MAU — direct buyer relationship beats agent intermediation.
CEO-founder direct decision authority — you can ship 'Buyer Adapter' as a category in one quarter, unlike Amazon.

engines.name: Buyer Adapter API
engines.flywheel_loop: Every agent query → SKU resolution → settlement → telemetry → adapter improves → more agents integrate

engines.name: Agent-Settlement Rails
engines.flywheel_loop: Agent-to-merchant payments → embedded financial primitives → margin compounds across GMV

engines.name: Shop Identity Graph
engines.flywheel_loop: Buyer identity layer for agents → trust signal → conversion uplift → identity becomes the moat

threats.name: Amazon Rufus + Buy-with-Prime
threats.key_fact: Vertically integrated agent + buyer base; can bypass Shopify entirely on commodity SKUs

threats.name: OpenAI ChatGPT Shopping
threats.key_fact: Direct merchant integrations being signed; could disintermediate Shop App

threats.name: Adept / Manus agent rails
threats.key_fact: Browser-agent vendors building neutral commerce rails

disqualifiers: You see AI agents as a feature instead of a category.
Product leadership doesn't want to expose buyer identity to agents.
Merchant ARR growth is high enough that the buyer-side wound doesn't feel urgent.
